meta.title: Nextjs Stack Authentication | JWT & User Management
meta.description: Pre-built Nextjs authentication with JWT, refresh tokens, password reset, and email verification. Express middleware and MongoDB schemas included.

hero.subheading: Complete JWT authentication system for Nextjs stack applications.

problem.heading: The Challenge
problem.description: Building secure authentication in Nextjs requires JWT token management, Express middleware, MongoDB user schemas, password hashing, session handling, and security best practices. It takes weeks to get right.

solution.heading: What's Included
points: Express routes for register, login, logout, password reset
JWT access and refresh token generation
MongoDB User schema with password hashing (bcrypt)
Password reset flow with expiring tokens

technicalDetails.heading: Technical Implementation
details: JWT tokens stored in httpOnly cookies
Refresh token rotation for security
Express middleware validates tokens on protected routes
MongoDB stores user credentials and tokens
bcrypt hashes passwords with salt rounds
Email service integration (SendGrid/Mailgun)
CORS configured for frontend-backend communication

codeExample.heading: Example: Protected Route Middleware
codeExample.description: Express middleware that validates JWT tokens on protected routes.

meta.title: Nextjs Stack Payment Integration | Stripe & PayPal
meta.description: Pre-built Stripe and PayPal integration for Nextjs. Express webhook handlers, MongoDB subscription schemas, and payment flows included.

hero.subheading: Stripe and PayPal integration built for Nextjs applications.

problem.heading: The Challenge
problem.description: Integrating Stripe with Nextjs requires Express endpoints for checkout, webhook handlers with signature verification, MongoDB subscription tracking, failed payment logic, and secure API key management.

solution.heading: What's Included
points: Express routes for Stripe Checkout session creation
MongoDB Subscription schema synced with Stripe
Failed payment recovery with email notifications
PayPal integration as alternative payment method

technicalDetails.heading: Technical Implementation
details: Stripe SDK integrated on backend
Express webhook route handles subscription events
MongoDB stores subscription and payment data
Frontend Checkout flow with Stripe Elements
Webhook events: checkout.session.completed, subscription updated, payment failed
Automatic user access gating based on subscription status
Environment variables for API keys

meta.title: Next.js Authentication Setup | NextAuth.js Complete
meta.description: Pre-built NextAuth.js authentication for Next.js 14+. OAuth providers, magic links, credentials, session management, and protected routes.

hero.subheading: Complete NextAuth.js implementation with multiple authentication methods.

problem.heading: The Challenge
problem.description: Setting up NextAuth.js requires configuring providers, database adapters, session strategies, protected routes, middleware, and handling edge cases. Documentation is scattered.

solution.heading: What's Included
points: NextAuth.js configured with multiple providers (Google, GitHub, Email)

technicalDetails.heading: Technical Implementation
details: NextAuth.js API route configured in app/api/auth/[...nextauth]
Multiple OAuth providers with environment variables
Prisma adapter stores sessions in database
Middleware checks authentication on protected routes
Server components can access session server-side
Client components use useSession hook
JWT strategy for stateless authentication
Database strategy for persistent sessions

meta.title: Next.js API Routes | Type-Safe REST API
meta.description: Pre-built Next.js API routes with TypeScript, Zod validation, error handling, and authentication middleware. Production-ready endpoints included.

hero.subheading: Type-safe API routes with validation, error handling, and authentication.

problem.heading: The Challenge
problem.description: Building production-ready API routes in Next.js requires type safety, request validation, error handling, authentication middleware, and proper response formatting. Easy to make mistakes.

solution.heading: What's Included
points: API routes in app/api with TypeScript

technicalDetails.heading: Technical Implementation
details: Route handlers with GET, POST, PUT, DELETE methods
Zod validates request body and query params
NextAuth middleware protects authenticated routes
Error handler returns consistent JSON responses
Type-safe with TypeScript throughout
Server actions for form submissions and mutations
Prisma client for database operations
Edge runtime for fast global responses
